reasoning: The informal statement is a standard Stein–Shakarchi exercise (Ch. 1, Ex. 13a) whose intended setting is a region (open connected set); without connectedness the claim is literally false (one can pick different constants on different components). The Lean signature adds `IsPreconnected Ω` which captures this "region" hypothesis, uses `IsOpen Ω` and `DifferentiableOn ℂ f Ω` for "holomorphic in an open set", and encodes "Re(f) is constant" as `∃ c : ℝ, ∀ z ∈ Ω, (f z).re = c`. The conclusion "f is constant" is encoded via `f a = f b` for universally quantified `a b : Ω`, which is equivalent to ∃c, ∀z∈Ω, f z = c (including trivially when Ω is empty). Under the standard mathematical reading of the exercise (Ω a region), the Lean statement matches exactly.
